                            - Edit = "Computer-Use Skill (Session 10) + cua-driver
Context
The repo has a mature Browser skill (code/browser/skill.py) with a visible cascade (Layer 1 extract → 2a deterministic → 2b a11y → 3 vision). There is no desktop/computer-use code today.
New dependency: cua-driver — a local desktop driver (Windows UI Automation, background/no-focus-steal) exposed as MCP stdio and CLI. It provides the assignment's start_recording, page (with electron_debugging_port), hotkey, get_window_state (capture modes ax / som / vision), and per-turn trajectory folders (turn-NNNNN/action.json, screenshot.png, app_state.json).
User choice: Wire cua-driver project-wide; each skill opts in via tools_allowed in agent_config.yaml. The computer skill is the primary cascade consumer; other skills (e.g. researcher) may use read-only cua tools when listed.
Primary OS: Windows. cua-driver is the official backend on Windows (not hand-rolled pyautogui / uiautomation).

cua-driver infrastructure (all skills, opt-in)
Install prerequisite (not pip)
cua-driver is a signed binary, installed via PowerShell (installation docs):
irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/trycua/cua/main/libs/cua-driver/scripts/install.ps1 | iex
cua-driver autostart          # Windows daemon in interactive session
cua-driver check_permissions  # UI Automation grants
Ship code/scripts/setup_cua_driver.ps1 wrapping the above for the course repo.
Daemon required for element-index workflows and start_recording (state lives in-process).
Shared Python wrapper — code/cua/
code/cua/
  __init__.py
  client.py       # CuaDriverClient — MCP stdio OR `cua-driver call` fallback
  tools.py        # Tool name constants + JSON schemas for mcp_runner merge
  recording.py    # start_recording / stop_recording wrappers
CuaDriverClient methods map 1:1 to MCP tools: launch_app, get_window_state, click, type_text, hotkey, page, start_recording, etc.
Dual MCP in code/mcp_runner.py
Today: single stdio server → code/mcp_server.py.
Change: When a skill's tools_allowed includes any tool prefixed cua_ (or listed in a CUA_TOOL_NAMES set), open a second stdio session:
StdioServerParameters(command="cua-driver", args=["mcp"])
Dispatch routing:
• web_search, fetch_url, … → eagv3 MCP (unchanged)
• cua_launch_app, cua_get_window_state, … → cua-driver MCP (prefixed names avoid collisions)
Alternative: pass through unprefixed cua tool names if the skill's allowlist is disjoint from eagv3 tools (current eagv3 has no click/hotkey).
Per-skill opt-in — code/agent_config.yaml

Full desktop stack
Computer skill does not go through mcp_runner tool loop (same pattern as browser); it calls CuaDriverClient internally.
code/skills.py tool payload
Extend tool_payload() to merge eagv3 schemas + cua schemas when skill lists cua tools.

Five-layer cascade (desktop) — backed by cua-driver
Cascade discipline stays visible in code (code/computer/skill.py); each layer is a thin module calling cua-driver with the right capture_mode / tool.

Maps to cua-driver capture modes (docs):
• Layer 2b AX → capture_mode=ax
• Layer 3 → capture_mode=vision (or som when AX empty but screenshot needed)
• Default daemon config can stay som; computer skill overrides per layer call
Electron path: use cua launch_app with electron_debugging_port + page tool — not a separate Playwright CDP client (cua owns CDP). Still Layer 2b, path=electron.
Escalation: same as browser — first useful layer wins; metadata.force_path: read | hotkey | electron | ax | vision.
Snapshot invariant (from cua quickstart): get_window_state before and after every action; element indices stale across turns.

Trajectory recording — native start_recording
Use cua-driver's MCP tool (docs) — not a custom recorder:
{
  "name": "start_recording",
  "arguments": {
    "output_dir": "state/sessions/<sid>/computer/trajectory_<ts>",
    "record_video": false
  }
}
Every subsequent cua action writes turn-NNNNN/ with action.json, screenshot.png, app_state.json, click.png (click family).
ComputerSkill.run():
1. start_recording(output_dir=...)
2. Run cascade
3. stop_recording on success/failure
4. Set ComputerOutput.trajectory_dir to that directory (evidence to submit)
Optional: merge gateway session id into a small manifest.json beside cua turns for cross-reference with /v1/cost/by_agent.

Package layout
code/cua/                    # NEW — shared cua-driver client (all opt-in skills)
  client.py
  tools.py
  recording.py

code/computer/               # NEW — cascade skill
  skill.py
  layer1_read.py
  layer2a_hotkey.py
  layer2b_electron.py        # cua page + CDP
  layer2b_ax.py              # element_index loop + V10Client chat
  layer3_vision.py           # vision capture_mode + V10Client vision
  fixtures/canvas_only.html
  scripts/launch_cursor_debug.ps1

code/scripts/setup_cua_driver.ps1
Reuse:
• code/browser/client.py — V10Client for LLM judgment in layers 2b/3 (same as browser)
• Do not add pyautogui, uiautomation, mss — cua-driver replaces them

Orchestrator integration
Schema — code/schemas.py
ComputerPath = Literal["read", "hotkey", "electron", "ax", "vision"]

class ComputerOutput(BaseModel):
    app: str
    goal: str
    path: ComputerPath
    turns: int = 0
    result: str | None = None
    actions: list[dict] = Field(default_factory=list)
    trajectory_dir: str   # cua-driver start_recording output_dir
Catalogue — computer entry + optional researcher cua tools (see table above)
Dispatch — code/skills.py if skill.name == "computer": branch (mirror browser)
Prompts — code/prompts/planner.md, code/prompts/computer.md

Dependencies — code/pyproject.toml
• No new pip packages for desktop automation (cua-driver is external binary)
• Existing: mcp, playwright (browser only), httpx
• Document cua-driver in code/COMPUTER_VALIDATION.md prerequisites section

Validation and evidence
code/COMPUTER_VALIDATION.md:
Check
Evidence
cua-driver MCP wired; researcher can opt into read-only tools
agent_config + manual tool call
Task 1 path=hotkey, 0 gateway vision
cost-by-agent
Task 2 path=electron, 0 vision
cost-by-agent
Task 3 path=vision
vision ledger + trajectory turn-*/screenshot.png
start_recording on every computer run
turn-00001/ folders under trajectory_dir
Five layers visible in code
layer*.py modules
# Prereqs
cd code; .\scripts\setup_cua_driver.ps1
cd ..\llm_gatewayV10; .\run.ps1

# Task 2: Cursor with CDP
cursor --remote-debugging-port=9222

cd ..\code
uv run python flow.py "Open Calculator and compute 847 times 293"
uv run python flow.py "In Cursor create notes/s10_evidence.txt with proof line"
uv run python flow.py "Open the canvas fixture and click inside the red circle"

Risk notes
Risk
Mitigation
cua-driver not on PATH
setup_cua_driver.ps1 + fail-fast in CuaDriverClient with install URL
UI Automation permissions
cua-driver check_permissions in setup script
Daemon not running
cua-driver autostart; computer skill checks daemon before cascade
Cursor CDP
electron_debugging_port on launch_app; document manual launch fallback
Dual MCP complexity
Prefix cua tools; unit-test dispatch routing
Browser skill unchanged
Web stays on Playwright; desktop on cua-driver

Out of scope
• No changes to code/flow.py graph logic
• No mandatory cua tools on planner/formatter (opt-in only)
• No record_video: true unless user requests (default false per assignment)
• Linux/macOS computer tasks in v1 (cua supports them; validate Windows first)
